hooks.command: echo 'Plugin openclinxr-slice-runner: OpenClaw-style / OpenClaw-inspired turn Stop. Prefer event-driven autonomy: run pnpm openclaw:run-next to select the next approved product slice and write only .openclinxr/openclaw/run-next-report.json. Use pnpm openclaw:watchdog only as a quiet local idle check; it must not append no-op heartbeat records to AUTONOMOUS_WORK_PLAN.md or other canonical state. Canonical state updates happen only after product code, verification evidence, or a real blocker changes. For real slice execution: acquire lease, consult repo role if useful, implement/verify, update state once, run post-slice/guards, release. This is not an external OpenClaw runtime.'; mkdir -p "${GROK_PLUGIN_DATA:-/tmp/grok-autonomy}"; echo "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ): slice-runner reminder emitted; use openclaw:run-next/watchdog, keep no-op logs local." >> "${GROK_PLUGIN_DATA:-/tmp/grok-autonomy}/slice-runner.log"; tail -5 "${GROK_PLUGIN_DATA:-/tmp/grok-autonomy}/slice-runner.log" || true

echo 'Plugin notification: use pnpm openclaw:run-next for OpenClaw-style / OpenClaw-inspired event-driven continuation. Optional pnpm openclaw:watchdog is quiet and local-report-only. Drift event? consult openclaw-drift-police before editing canonical state.'

echo 'UserPromptSubmit (OpenClaw-style autonomy): if prompt mentions heartbeat/orchestrate/next slice, prefer pnpm openclaw:run-next; then lease + execute only a real selected slice. No no-op heartbeat entries in canonical state.'
